_comment: Baseline numbers measured 2026-04-23. Captures micro-benchmarks (B1) of sprint-21. Hardware: developer local machine. Numbers are per-operation mean unless noted. `hz` = operations per second; per-op cost = 1/hz. Where a benchmark runs N internal iterations, per-call cost = (1/hz) / N.

chemistry_read_outside_scope.note: Essentially zero overhead vs plain JS — accessor getter is V8-inlinable

chemistry_write_outside_scope.note: Framework write is comparable to plain write; additional react() call is near-free when update$ is undefined (not mounted)

scope_cost.note: Per-tracked-read cost dominated by $symbolize() on the read value

instantiation.note: 1000 chemicals instantiated in <2ms — well within scaling budget

verdict_v1.accessor_cost: PASS — no measurable overhead outside scope
verdict_v1.scope_cost: PASS — 56μs for 10-read scope well under 4ms render budget
verdict_v1.instantiation: PASS — <2μs per chemical, extrapolates to 2ms for 1000
verdict_v1.overall: SHIP-READY for the GitHub Pages static site. Micro-level overhead is in the noise for typical UI workloads.

signals_for_v2_trigger.symbolize_dominates_handler_time: Not yet measured — component benchmarks (B2) will surface. Threshold: >40% of event-handler time in $symbolize.
signals_for_v2_trigger.instantiation_cliff: Not observed at 20 fields. If fields/chemical exceeds ~100, measure again.
signals_for_v2_trigger.scope_cost_at_many_reads: Linear in reads × $symbolize cost. For views reading many fields, expect scope cost in ms range.

macro_render_scaling._context: These are INITIAL RENDER numbers — first paint from cold. Events after initial render do NOT re-render the whole tree; see locality section below.
macro_render_scaling.comparison_baseline: React alone renders 1000 rows in ~25ms (js-framework-benchmark). Our overhead factor ≈7x. Solid ~7ms.
macro_render_scaling.note: Initial render cost proportional to total chemicals rendered. This is one-time load cost, not per-event.

_optimization_applied: 2026-04-23: switched Scope snapshot/diff from $symbolize (string serialize + string compare) to deep-clone + equivalent() (structural walk with early exit). 2-4x faster on microbenchmarks; 19x faster on fine-grained composition.

locality_per_event._context: Cost of an event in an ALREADY-RENDERED tree. This is what app-responsiveness actually depends on.
locality_per_event.scaling_factor_200_vs_10: 1.09x — flat. Re-render cost is independent of total tree size.
locality_per_event.verification: Render counter confirms: only target leaf re-renders; siblings and container do NOT re-render when event fires on a leaf.
locality_per_event.implication: Re-render cost scales with MUTATING SUBTREE, not total tree size. An app with 500 chemicals costs ~150μs per event, not 500 × 150μs.

granularity_tradeoff._context: Same DOM (50 cells): one chemical with 50 items vs 50 chemicals with 1 item each.
granularity_tradeoff.winner: GRANULAR — 7.68x cheaper per event now that scope snapshot/diff is cheap.
granularity_tradeoff.design_guidance: Prefer granular composition when items update independently. Each chemical is cheap (~250μs per event). Monolithic still appropriate when items update together.

verdict_by_app_shape.static_site_few_dynamic_components: EXCELLENT — initial render cheap, per-event cost negligible
verdict_by_app_shape.real_app_hundreds_of_chemicals_most_idle: EXCELLENT — per-event cost stays at 170μs-300μs regardless of total count
verdict_by_app_shape.real_app_bulk_simultaneous_updates_500pl: NOTICEABLE — batch mutations that trigger full-tree re-renders take 70ms+
verdict_by_app_shape.large_app_rare_edge_case: STATIC ANALYSIS USEFUL for further optimization
